description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves reveal a dark, possibly hollow or deeply recessed interior, contrasting with the speckled, granite-like stone material on the upper surface. The block's sides are solid and smooth, with the stone pattern only visible on the top, while the vertical faces are uniformly dark, suggesting either a different material or a shadowed recess. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it may serve a functional or decorative purpose in an urban environment. Its robust construction and heavy appearance suggest it is designed to act as a barrier or obstacle.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on top, revealing dark recesses beneath a speckled granite-like surface. The sides are solid and dark, emphasizing the ribbed, heavy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone block's grooved top surface and solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the stone pattern visible only on the top.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves and the contrast between the stone top and dark sides.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative or functional barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the stone surface appears clean and unworn, with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a repeating barrier element
